summary: Descriptive overview of childcustody in substantive law: best interests standard, joint vs sole, relocation.
key_points: best interests standard is a fundamental concept in family law.
Legal standards for childcustody are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine childcustody in litigation.
The doctrine of childcustody has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding childcustody requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
